reasoning:
Focus area 'vulnerabilities': The requested metrics require joins (host metadata + detections), time-series snapshots, consistent time-grain normalization, and derived fields (is_open, is_critical, days_open, numeric CVSS casting and high-CVSS flag). These require stable, materialized gold models per data source and per time grain so metrics can be computed by simple SELECTs against gold tables without rejoining silver tables. Following the medallion and source-separation rules, separate gold models are required for Qualys (host detections/hosts/applications) and Snyk (issues). Each time grain (daily/weekly/monthly) will be a separate gold model; weekly models will reference daily gold models, monthly will reference weekly models.

Focus area 'asset_inventory': The requested asset_inventory metrics require filtered time-series snapshots, joins between qualys silver tables (host detections, hosts, applications), and aggregations at daily/weekly/monthly grains (counts, trends, average days open, unique software counts). These are not simple one-table ad-hoc selects against a single silver table — they require: (a) stable daily snapshots that normalize snapshot_date and host context, (b) weekly and monthly aggregations for trends and reporting, and (c) application-level normalization for licensing and exposure metrics. Following the Medallion and source-separation rules, we will create qualys-source gold models only (the metrics are qualys-focused). We will create a hierarchical set of gold models per time grain (daily incremental snapshots built from qualys silver tables; weekly models that aggregate from daily gold models; monthly models that aggregate from weekly gold models). Each gold model is scoped to the asset_inventory focus area and to a single data source (qualys).

specifications.name: gold_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily_snapshot
specifications.description: Focus Area: vulnerabilities; Data Source: qualys; Time Grain: daily (foundation). Build from silver tables: qualys_host_detections, qualys_hosts, qualys_applications. Join qualys_host_detections.host_id = qualys_hosts.id to bring host attributes (ip, os). Left-join qualys_applications on host_id when application-level context is available (not required for provided metrics but included for lineage). Transformations: normalize a snapshot_date (DATE) from last_found_datetime (DATE_TRUNC day), derive is_open boolean from status (status values indicating open), derive is_critical boolean (severity indicates CRITICAL / or severity == '5' depending on the raw values), compute days_open = datediff(current_date, last_found_datetime) for quick backlog calculations, cast/normalize severity to canonical string. Output: a daily row-level snapshot of each detection with host metadata and derived fields. This model supports all Qualys-focused metrics by enabling simple aggregations (COUNT, AVG) and trends over snapshot_date without joining back to silver. How to compute each metric from columns in this model: - vuln_by_asset_criticality:by_host and vuln_by_asset_criticality:by_host_critical_vuln_count_per_host: SELECT host_id, ip, os, COUNT(*) AS critical_vuln_count WHERE is_critical = true AND is_open = true GROUP BY host_id, ip, os. - vuln_by_asset_criticality:by_host_30_day_trend_of_critical_vulnerabilities: use snapshot_date and is_critical/is_open to count per day over 30 days. - vuln_by_asset_criticality:by_os: GROUP BY os, severity or is_critical to compute counts, plus weekly trend by grouping snapshot_date by week. - vuln_fix_backlog_by_team:by_host and vuln_fix_backlog_by_team:trend_over_90_days: filter is_open = true; aggregate COUNT(*) by host_id and AVG(days_open) to compute backlog and average days open; use snapshot_date for 90-day trend.

source_columns.usage: filter/time grain (derive snapshot_date); compute days_open; trend grouping
direct mapping (vulnerability identifier); optional for drilldown
filter (severity), derive is_critical, grouping by severity
filter (determine open vs closed), derive is_open
direct mapping; grouping (by OS), used by by_os metric
join key (optional left join for app-level context)

specifications.name: gold_qualys_vulnerabilities_weekly_snapshot
specifications.description: Focus Area: vulnerabilities; Data Source: qualys; Time Grain: weekly. Aggregates from daily foundation: ref('gold_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily_snapshot'). Prefer referencing the daily model for efficiency. Aggregations: compute weekly counts and weekly averages per grouping. Transformations: DATE_TRUNC(snapshot_date, 'week') as week_start; for critical vuln counts compute SUM(CASE WHEN is_critical AND is_open THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as critical_vuln_count_week; for backlog compute AVG(days_open) per host per week and open_vuln_count_week. This model supports weekly trend KPIs and reduces compute for monthly layer.

source_columns.usage: filtering and conditional aggregation (weekly critical counts)
aggregation (AVG days_open per host per week)

Transformations: DATE_TRUNC(week_start, 'month') or DATE_TRUNC(snapshot_date,'month') as month_start; compute monthly critical counts and monthly backlog averages per host/os. Materialized as a table for stable monthly reporting. This model supports monthly reports on critical vuln counts by host and OS and long-term backlog trends.

specifications.name: gold_snyk_issues_daily_snapshot
specifications.description: Focus Area: vulnerabilities; Data Source: snyk; Time Grain: daily. Build from silver table: snyk_issues. Normalize nested fields: extract v1.issue.cvssScore (cast to numeric as cvss_score), v1.issue.language as issue_language, v1.issue.package as issue_package, v1.issue.publicationTime as publication_time. Derive is_high_cvss boolean (cvss_score >= 7.0). Normalize snapshot_date from ingestion/published time (or current date) for daily grain. This daily snapshot provides row-level Snyk issue data for computing CVSS distributions and high-CVSS counts by package/language without re-parsing nested structures every time.

source_columns.usage: filter (present and numeric), cast to numeric for cvss_score; grouping (cvss distribution)
direct mapping (publication timestamp); optional time-based analysis
direct mapping for vulnerability version ranges (optional)

specifications.name: gold_snyk_issues_weekly_snapshot
specifications.description: Focus Area: vulnerabilities; Data Source: snyk; Time Grain: weekly. Aggregates from daily foundation: ref('gold_snyk_issues_daily_snapshot'). Compute week_start = DATE_TRUNC(snapshot_date, 'week'). Aggregations: count_high_cvss_week = SUM(CASE WHEN is_high_cvss THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) grouped by issue_language, issue_package, or organization_id; distribution of cvss_score by week (histogram buckets or percentile). Materialization incremental for efficient weekly refresh. This model supports weekly language/package CVSS distributions and counts.

specifications.name: gold_qualys_host_detections_daily_snapshot
specifications.description: Focus area: asset_inventory; data_source: qualys; time_grain: daily; entity: host_detections. Foundation daily incremental snapshot built from qualys_host_detections joined to qualys_hosts to attach host context. Use qualys_host_detections as the primary row-level detection source; left-join qualys_hosts on qualys_host_detections.host_id = qualys_hosts.id to bring ip/os/first_found_date. Normalize a snapshot_date = DATE_TRUNC('day', last_found_datetime) or model load date if last_found_datetime is null. Output is one row per detection per snapshot_date with host attributes and detection attributes so all vulnerability and backlog metrics (counts, trends, average-days calculations) can be computed by simple SELECTs (COUNT / AVG / GROUP BY) on this table without re-joining silver. Calculation guidance: critical vuln counts use severity='CRITICAL' and status IN (open statuses) and GROUP BY host_id or os; 30-day and 90-day trends use snapshot_date windows and counts of rows per snapshot_date; average days open is computed as AVG(DATEDIFF(day, last_found_datetime, snapshot_date)) for rows with status=open. This model supports metrics: vuln_by_asset_criticality:by_host, vuln_by_asset_criticality:by_host_critical_vuln_count_per_host, vuln_by_asset_criticality:by_host_30_day_trend_of_critical_vulnerabilities, vuln_by_asset_criticality:by_os, vuln_by_asset_criticality:by_os_critical_vuln_count_by_os, vuln_by_asset_criticality:by_os_weekly_trend_by_os, vuln_fix_backlog_by_team:by_host, vuln_fix_backlog_by_team:by_host_open_vulnerability_count_per_host, vuln_fix_backlog_by_team:by_host_average_days_open, vuln_fix_backlog_by_team:trend_over_90_days, vuln_fix_backlog_by_team:trend_over_90_days_open_vulnerability_trend_90d, vuln_fix_backlog_by_team:trend_over_90_days_change_in_backlog_size, asset_agent_coverage_hosts_without_recent_detections.

source_columns.usage: filter (time windows), derive snapshot_date, compute days open (datediff), trend buckets
direct mapping (vulnerability identifier), optional grouping/unique counts
filter (e.g., severity='CRITICAL'), grouping (by severity), KPI calculation
filter (open vs closed), define backlog (open vulnerabilities)
direct mapping, grouping (by OS), KPI by OS
filter / host inventory context (support hosts without recent detections)

specifications.name: gold_qualys_hosts_daily_snapshot
specifications.description: Focus area: asset_inventory; data_source: qualys; time_grain: daily; entity: hosts. Daily incremental host inventory snapshot built from qualys_hosts. Normalize snapshot_date = DATE_TRUNC('day', first_found_date) or ingestion date when necessary. Output is one row per host per day with host attributes (id, ip, os, first_found_date, organization_id, connection_id). This table supports agent coverage metrics and helps detect hosts without recent detections when joined or compared to the host_detections daily snapshot. Weekly/monthly aggregates can reference this model for inventory counts and site-level coverage.

specifications.name: gold_qualys_applications_daily_snapshot
specifications.description: Focus area: asset_inventory; data_source: qualys; time_grain: daily; entity: applications. Daily incremental snapshot of installed applications built from qualys_applications joined with qualys_hosts for host context. Primary use: software licensing compliance, unique software counts, EOL detection (when version present), proxy for open ports/exposure via application counts per host. Normalize snapshot_date as model ingestion date (qualys_applications does not always include discovery timestamp in available schema), and record one row per (host_id, app_name, version) per snapshot to allow weekly/monthly deltas such as 'new installs in last 7 days' by comparing snapshot windows. Supports metrics: asset_software_licensing_compliance, asset_software_licensing_compliance_unique_software_count, asset_software_licensing_compliance_eol_software_count_where_identifiable, asset_open_ports_summary:by_application, asset_open_ports_summary:by_application_application_count_per_host_proxy_for_exposure.

application name normalization and grouping (unique software identification)
application version for unique software identity and EOL detection when version is available

host OS context for application rows (useful to identify OS-specific software concentrations)

specifications.name: gold_qualys_host_detections_weekly_snapshot
specifications.description: Focus area: asset_inventory; data_source: qualys; time_grain: weekly; entity: host_detections. Weekly aggregation that references the daily snapshot for efficiency. Source: gold_qualys_host_detections_daily_snapshot. Aggregate daily rows into week (e.g., DATE_TRUNC('week', snapshot_date)) and produce weekly counts and summary fields per group (host, os, severity). Compute weekly open vulnerability counts per host/os, weekly average days open (AVG(days_since_last_found) for open items), and weekly snapshots for 7/30/90-day trend windows. This model is incremental (weekly) and optimized for trend dashboards. Metrics supported: weekly variants of vuln_by_asset_criticality (by_os_weekly_trend_by_os), vuln_fix_backlog_by_team:trend_over_90_days (roll-up), and host-level weekly counts.

aggregate AVG(days_since_last_found) for weekly 'average days open'

Materialized as a table for reporting.

source_columns.usage: monthly aggregation of unique software counts

expected_columns.name: connection_id
expected_columns.description: Qualys connection id for multi-tenant filtering

expected_columns.name: month_start_date
expected_columns.description: Month bucket (DATE_TRUNC('month', week_start_date)).

expected_columns.name: name
expected_columns.description: Application name (product/vendor) used to aggregate license coverage and unlicensed concentrations.

expected_columns.name: version
expected_columns.description: Application version used for unique software identity and EOL counts (when identifiable).

expected_columns.name: monthly_unique_software_count
expected_columns.description: COUNT DISTINCT(name,version) aggregated for the month (per host or per site depending on GROUP BY).

expected_columns.name: monthly_eol_software_count
expected_columns.description: COUNT of applications flagged as EOL in the month (where version and EOL mapping available).

specifications.name: gold_qualys_hosts_monthly_snapshot
specifications.description: Focus area: asset_inventory; data_source: qualys; time_grain: monthly; entity: hosts. Monthly host inventory built from weekly host snapshots (gold_qualys_hosts_weekly_snapshot) to support monthly coverage reporting and long-term trends on hosts without detections. Materialized as a table.

source_columns.usage: aggregate monthly counts of hosts without detections (coverage gaps)

expected_columns.name: connection_id
expected_columns.description: Qualys connection id for multi-tenant filtering

expected_columns.name: month_start_date
expected_columns.description: Month bucket (DATE_TRUNC('month', week_start_date)).

expected_columns.name: organization_id
expected_columns.description: Organization/site id for grouping coverage metrics.

expected_columns.name: monthly_total_hosts
expected_columns.description: COUNT of hosts in inventory for the month (denominator for coverage %).

expected_columns.name: monthly_hosts_without_recent_detections
expected_columns.description: COUNT of hosts lacking recent detections aggregated to the month (used to highlight coverage gaps).
